scope: custom: combat -- brand-new armour substrate (material+coverage axes, mitigation matrix), Build-12
note: Brand-new armour system, bottom-up (Jordan design grant 2026-05-30). REPRODUCES the canonical skeleton exactly (4 tiers; STR-min 0/2/3/4; per-action Stamina drain +0/+0/+1/+2; flat Health +4/+6/+8; the W1+W5 weapon-vs-armour table) -- NO canonical value changed. Adds the structure underneath: the weapon-vs-armour table re-read as an (attack-head x material) MITIGATION MATRIX (calibrated so effective_mod = base_vs_none - mitigation reproduces every ratified row -- self-test check 1), plus two new axes (material none/cloth/mail/plate -> matrix; coverage partial/full -> gap exposure) with the four tiers as presets. Costs route through ratified channels: Stamina per-action drain (canonical), flat Health (canonical), STR-min (canonical), + a NEW sigma-tempo penalty (the only new combat lever, flagged for ratification). Self-test 7/7. TOP-DOWN VALIDATION (combat-sim): weapon-head vs plate = blunt fells in 3.0 / point 5.5 via gaps / cut 6.2 deflected (exactly history); coverage partial<full; mail anti-cut>anti-thrust. CORRECTED FINDING: the design's 'fatigue erodes plate in a long duel' claim was FALSIFIED (symmetric fatigue cancels; progressive penalty and kiting both fail) -- plate is ~95% dominant 1v1 vs unarmoured, which is HISTORICALLY CORRECT; its real counters are blunt + thrust-to-gaps + grappling (a future mechanic), not endurance. Fatigue re-scoped to a cost/constraint (action budget, wield-floor, campaign attrition), not a duel equaliser. The progressive-fatigue idea was tested and REJECTED; the module uses the canonical per-action drain only. Class A = the reproduced canonical skeleton + W1/W5 table. Class C = the matrix calibration + coverage factor + the sigma-tempo seed. Reuses M1 LEVEL_SIGMA + wa2 weapon axes. PROPOSAL -- Jordan-vetoable.

entries.quoted_text: None/Light/Medium/Heavy. STR mins 0/2/3/4. Drain per action +0/+0/+1/+2. Equipment flat Health +4 leather/+6 chain/+8 plate. Reproduced verbatim -- no retcon.
cut_light 3/2/1/0; cut_heavy 6/4/2/0; point_light 3/3/2/1 (W5); blunt_light 3/3/2/2 (W1); blunt_heavy 5/5/4/3 (W1). The matrix reproduces these exactly.

effective_mod = base_vs_none - mitigation[head][material]. cut stopped progressively (cloth/mail/plate), point finds gaps vs plate, blunt transmits. Calibrated so the four tier-presets reproduce the ratified table; structure matches arms-and-armour history.
partial coverage protects the vital zone only -> half the body exposed -> applied_mitigation = matrix x coverage_factor. Avoids a per-body-part hit-location system (over-engineering guard).
NEW: armour imposes a modest sigma-tempo/initiative penalty scaled by weight (plate is a step slow, NOT immobilising -- historically accurate). The only new combat lever; flagged for ratification.
